data.description:
Update the skills installation, doctor command, and doctor --fix to recognize and work with Codex project-level skills located in ./.agents/skills directory, alongside user-level skills. This enables projects to define their own custom skills that are version-controlled and shareable with team members.

## Implementation Plan

### Summary
Add `.agents/skills` as a project-level skill location for Codex, giving it the same project+user pattern that OpenCode already has.

### Current → Target State
- Codex currently: user-only at `~/.codex/skills`
- Codex target: project `.agents/skills` (preferred) + user `~/.codex/skills` (fallback)

### File Changes

**`src/managed_skills.rs` (~2 changes)**
1. `location_candidates()`: Add project-level candidate `repo_root.join(".agents")` with `LocationScope::Project` before the user-level `~/.codex` entry.
2. `expected_root_hint()`: Update Codex hint to `".agents or ~/.codex"`.

**`src/managed_skills_tests.rs` (~6 test updates)**
1. New: `install_prefers_project_codex_location_when_agents_dir_exists()`
2. New: `codex_falls_back_to_user_root_when_agents_dir_is_absent()`
3. New: `doctor_detects_project_level_codex_skills()`
4. New: `doctor_fix_reconciles_project_level_codex_skills()`
5. Update: `locations_detect_supported_roots_for_all_tools()` — Codex now 2 locations
6. Update: `doctor_checks_warn_when_roots_are_missing()` — hint text changes

### No Changes Needed
- `managed_skills_inventory.rs`, `managed_skills_state.rs`, `managed_skills_output.rs` — location-agnostic
- `doctor.rs`, `doctor_fix.rs` — routing handles `skills_codex` already
- `cli_skills.rs` — CLI args unchanged

### Complexity: Low
Pattern already exists for OpenCode. Fallback chain in `doctor_location()` ensures backward compatibility.

### Risks
- Existing user-level installs unaffected (fallback chain preserves `~/.codex` when `.agents` absent)
- Uninstall iterates all locations, correctly cleaning both project and user-level skills

### Test Strategy
- Unit tests for location resolution with/without `.agents`
- Doctor check/fix tests for project-level paths
- Existing tests unchanged (no `.agents` dirs created)
- `make sanity` must pass
